title: Looking for small Eva figures need suggestions Heroclix
display_title: Looking for small Eva figures need suggestions Heroclix
wikitext:
[]: Looking for small Eva figures, need suggestions [Heroclix]             Quote Post by commandercool » Sun Jul 07, 2013 4:44 pm I'm looking for some figures and am not sure if anything of this description actually exists or where I would go to buy them if they did. I figured you guys would have some ideas, so please let me know if anything comes to mind. I play the miniatures game Heroclix, which is like a board game where all of the pieces are figurines of super heroes. There's a Pacific Rim set upcoming for the game and I'd like to snap the miniatures off of their bases are replace them with Evas, but am having trouble finding anything the right size. The figures should be 2.5-3 inches (6-6.5 centimeters) and fit on a 1.5 inch diameter round base. Ideally I'm looking for Unit 00, Unit 01, and Unit 02, with Unit 06, Unit 08, Sachiel, Ramiel, Bardiel, and Zeruel as secondary interests. Design doesn't really matter that much, as long as they're reasonably good-looking. I'd prefer to avoid spending a ton of money, and would like to be able to purchase these individually and in multiples. Does something like this exist? I've been looking around Ebay and have found a few promising Gashapon figures, but they require me to buy things I don't want as well as part of the set and many of the listings aren't clear on the size of the figures. Bonus points if anyone knows of Gurren Lagann and Gunbuster figures in this scale as well. Any suggestions or information are appreciated, and thanks for reading.

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Mon Jul 08, 2013 5:21 pm The Fraulein Heroine series has some nice figures of Evas 01, 02, 6, and 13, and if I remember, all of them except 13 come on a base due to their poses. You can find them on ebay for about 10-12 bucks each. The Neo-Act gashapon series has the only Eva 08 I've seen, but you might also try Bandai's High Grade figures. Hope that helps!

[]:             Quote Post by commandercool » Mon Jul 08, 2013 5:56 pm Hmm, all of those look pretty good. The Bandai Ultimate Action figures have about the right selection of characters and seem to be the only ones that are reliably purchaseable individually. I think I'll order a few of those and see what they look like in person. The Fraulein Heroine ones look possibly the nicest, but I'm concerned that the bases would cover up the states on the Heroclix bases I plan on swapping them onto, which would defeat the purpose. I'm also having trouble finding a place where I can buy them non-blind and individually. But having names for these lines makes searching a lot easier. Thanks for the help.

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Mon Jul 08, 2013 6:12 pm You're welcome! I was actually in the market for that sort of thing myself. Glad I could be of assistance The Ultimate Action figures DO look amazing for the comparative prices. I just got a set of 5 (Evas 1-3, Orange Israfel and a Launcher) off Ebay for 16 bucks, and it looks like Amazon has the Evas, Sachiel and Shamshel for 10 each. Amazon and Ebay are both good choices for finding the figures individually, but I'm a bit leery of the ebay listings since most of them are from Hong Kong

[]:             Quote Post by commandercool » Mon Jul 08, 2013 6:17 pm I was just coming back to ask about Hong Kong sellers, actually. Looks like the Ultimate Action Series 1 is perfect for what I'm looking for, but the only places I've been able to find it for sale so far are sketchy Ebay sellers and an even sketchier store. Series 3 also looks interesting although I haven't been able to find a good picture of the Beast Mode Unit 02 figure. It looks kind of questionable in the stock picture, although that might just be the angle. That one has a Japanese seller on Ebay for a decent price, which I assume is less risky. Either set nets me Evas 0-2 and a high-profile Angel, so I'd be fine with either after a little more research. No luck at all so far on the Gunbuster/Gurren Lagann front though. Edit: Closer inspection seems to indicate that Series 3 may in fact me a different set of figures and the one I'm looking at may be mislabeled. I'm talking about the Rebuild 2.0 set with Beast Unit 02, Unit 00 with a missile, Unit 01, and Zeruel. Back to research I guess to make sure I'm looking for the right thing.

[]:             Quote Post by PainXIII12 » Tue Jul 09, 2013 8:04 pm i was thinking of the same idea of evangelion heroclix! please post the dials!

[]:             Quote Post by commandercool » Thu Jul 11, 2013 10:45 am I'm using the Pacific Rim figures that aren't quite out yet. These are allegedly their dials, although I suspect there are a few mistakes in the notation. I'm almost done making cards for them, and I have Gipsy Danger as Unit 02, Cherno Alpha as Unit 01, Crimson Typhoon as Unit 00, and Slattern as Zeruel. If it goes well I'll be finding characters to match up to the rest of the figures. I ordered the capsule figures a couple of days ago, so I'll let you know how everything looks once I get it all assembled.

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Fri Jul 12, 2013 11:15 pm I got my set of Ultimate Action figures in the mail today and got everything set up: [url]https://www.dropbox.com/s/6ve1a35xgy0seeq/Ultimate%20Action%20Evas.JPG[/url] They're pretty amazing for their size and price. At the top of the pylons, they're 4 1/2-5 inches tall, plus the three evas have multiple points of ball-joint articulation, two sets of extra hands, and Units 01 and 2 came with their own stands!. Israfel's got a very slight bit of articulation in its arms and I doubt it would fit on a heroclix base with that wide stance, but it's still really cool! Definitely looking for more of these babies.

[]:             Quote Post by commandercool » Sat Jul 13, 2013 9:37 pm BlueBasilisk wrote: I got my set of Ultimate Action figures in the mail today and got everything set up: [url]https://www.dropbox.com/s/6ve1a35xgy0seeq/Ultimate%20Action%20Evas.JPG[/url] They're pretty amazing for their size and price. At the top of the pylons, they're 4 1/2-5 inches tall, plus the three evas have multiple points of ball-joint articulation, two sets of extra hands, and Units 01 and 2 came with their own stands!. Israfel's got a very slight bit of articulation in its arms and I doubt it would fit on a heroclix base with that wide stance, but it's still really cool! Definitely looking for more of these babies.

[]: bbts             Quote Post by whiemeat » Thu Aug 15, 2013 9:59 am check out the big bad toy store
